mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, monetary policy becomes restrictive enough to suppress aggregate demand and corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when FEDFUNDS crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market tightening, which drives wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, rising retail sales and housing starts signal peak consumer leverage and demand, which temporarily boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in >60% of observations during late-cycle phases, or the 5-day forward return correlation with demand proxies drops below 0.01 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly increases corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, economic momentum is insufficient to sustain corporate profit expansion, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when tested against a 2.0 percent cutoff in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with accelerating inflation indicates a restrictive real policy stance that crushes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive coefficient in regression analysis, or 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of cases when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a declining unemployment rate signals successful labor market stabilization and rising consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in >60% of observations during recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy (Rank IC) falls below 0.03 when isolated to this regime.
